question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a validStartTimestamp set to a future time, causing subscribers that enforce transaction ordering by validStartTimestamp to accept a transaction that should not yet be valid, enabling premature execution of time-locked operations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a validStartTimestamp set to a past time that predates the topic's creation, causing subscribers to believe the transaction was submitted before the topic existed and corrupting the topic's historical integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a message byte array of exactly maxPageSize * maxMessageSize bytes, causing poll() to load the entire page into memory at once and trigger an OutOfMemoryError in the Flux.fromStream() pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes total.get() to overflow (via AtomicLong wrapping) when incremented in onNext(), causing isComplete()'s limitHit check (filter.getLimit() == total.get()) to never trigger and the polling loop to run indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a topicId that encodes a SQL injection payload in its EntityId string representation, and if findByFilter() ever falls back to a native query path, cause unauthorized database access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a consensusTimestamp that is 1 nanosecond before the filter's endTime, causing it to be included in the last page of results, and if this message has a tampered runningHash, it corrupts the final state of the hash chain delivered to the subscriber?
